perturbation.original_text: all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.
perturbation.changed_text: all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall be interpreted to mean only ANRA. The definition shall not extend to VNBZ unless explicitly stated.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the interpretation of "Company" or "Companies" to solely ANRA, and explicitly excluding VNBZ unless otherwise stated, this change introduces an in-text contradiction. Previous sections and potentially subsequent sections might rely on the original, broader definition of "Company", which included both VNBZ and ANRA, especially when delineating responsibilities, obligations, or asset distributions related to both entities post-closing. This new, narrow definition would create ambiguity, potentially leading to disputes regarding the intended scope of clauses referencing "Company", especially if those sections where not explicitly modified to reflect this exclusion.
perturbation.location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement a.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the Nissin Parties agree to cause each of VNBJ and VNBZ to comply with Sections 4.3.2 and 4.3.3 of the JV Agreement with respect to the indemnification or reimbursement, as applicable, of all Directors and Officers (except those individuals resigning pursuant to Section 6.2.6 of the VNBJ SPA and Section 6.2.6 of the VNBZ SPA) with respect to any Liabilities arising prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Company' to only ANRA, explicitly excluding VNBZ unless otherwise stated. This contradicts the provided section, which states that 'the Nissin Parties agree to cause each of VNBJ and VNBZ to comply with Sections 4.3.2 and 4.3.3 of the JV Agreement.' Since VNBZ is now excluded from the default definition of 'Company,' it creates ambiguity regarding whether VNBZ is still bound by the JV agreement in other sections, specifically Sections 4.3.2 and 4.3.3. This falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' type, as the altered definition omits VNBZ from being included, creating a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: d.Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall not release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: d.Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to the Veoneer Parties shall not release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement. This holds only for breaches committed by the closing of VNBJ.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting that the termination of JV Agreement with respect to VNBZ shall not release any Veoneer Party, and any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBZ Closing, as applicable. And only restricting that the breaches that shall be considerated are the ones committed by the closing of VNBJ, an in-text contradiction is created. The change contradicts other clauses that refer generally to the liabilities prior to both VNBJ and VNBZ closings. By limiting the liability clause to only VNBJ closing, it creates ambiguity around the scope of liabilities related to VNBZ closing, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement d.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the Nissin Parties agree to cause each of VNBJ and VNBZ to comply with Sections 4.3.2 and 4.3.3 of the JV Agreement with respect to the indemnification or reimbursement, as applicable, of all Directors and Officers (except those individuals resigning pursuant to Section 6.2.6 of the VNBJ SPA and Section 6.2.6 of the VNBZ SPA) with respect to any Liabilities arising prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the termination of the JV agreement with respect to VNBJ and VNBZ shall not release any party from liability for breaches before the VNBJ or VNBZ closing. The changed text removes the reference to VNBZ and only considers breaches committed by the closing of VNBJ. The contradicted text refers to compliance regarding liabilities arising prior to both VNBJ and VNBZ closings. Thus, by removing VNBZ from the clause regarding the termination of the JV agreement and only mentioning VNBJ, the Changed Text creates a contradiction to the Contradicted Text. The modification introduces an omission that creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections, based on the Perturbation Definition provided. This omission regarding VNBZ could lead to different interpretations and therefore creating a contradiction in the document.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall only become effective upon the VNBJ Closing with respect to Article 1.1 and the VNBZ Closing with respect to Article 1.2, and shall terminate without any force or effect in the event that the VNBJ SPA and the VNBZ SPA are terminated in accordance with the terms thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall only become effective upon the VNBJ Closing with respect to Article 1.1, and shall terminate without any force or effect in the event that the VNBJ SPA is terminated in accordance with the terms thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the VNBZ Closing and VNBZ SPA elements, the amendment's effectiveness and termination conditions are now solely tied to VNBJ. This directly contradicts the Recitals and potentially other sections of the agreement that explicitly define the agreement's scope as encompassing both the VNBJ and VNBZ transactions. This omission creates uncertainty as to the enforceability and validity of the agreement concerning VNBZ, even if the VNBZ SPA is executed successfully.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (F)Honda Motor Co., Ltd. ("Honda"), as of the Effective Date, Nissin and Veoneer AB entered into those certain (1) VNBJ Share Purchase Agreement, pursuant to which Veoneer AB is selling, and Honda and Nissin are purchasing, all of Veoneer AB's outstanding shares in VNBJ (the "VNBJ SPA," and the consummation of the transactions pursuant to the terms of the VNBJ SPA, the "VNBJ Closing") and (2) VNBZ Share Purchase Agreement, pursuant to which Veoneer AB is selling, and Honda and Nissin are purchasing, all of Veoneer AB's equity interests in VNBZ (the "VNBZ SPA," and the consummation of the transactions pursuant to the terms of the VNBZ SPA, the "VNBZ Closing"); and
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits any mention of the VNBZ transaction, focusing solely on VNBJ. This directly contradicts the Recital (F), which explicitly states that the agreement encompasses both VNBJ and VNBZ transactions. By removing the VNBZ elements, the amendment's effectiveness and termination are solely linked to VNBJ, creating a clear inconsistency with the Recital's description of the agreement's scope, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
